title: Segfault in `_locale.nl_langinfo` in a process started by `os.execvpe`
author: devdanzin
body:
It's possible to segfault PyPy by calling `_locale.nl_langinfo` in a process started by `os.execvpe`. Sorry if this is an invalid use case, I wasn't able to reduce the segfault to a single process.

```python
from os import execvpe
from sys import executable

code = """
import _locale

_locale.nl_langinfo(7)
"""
filename = 'source__locale.py'

with open(filename, 'w') as source:
    source.write(code)

arguments = [executable, '-u', filename]
execvpe(arguments[0], arguments, {})
```

`pypy -VV`:
```
Python 3.10.14 (b928ea00cb58, Nov 22 2024, 04:32:31)
[PyPy 7.3.18-alpha0 with GCC 10.2.1 20210130 (Red Hat 10.2.1-11)]
```
Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.

comments.author: mattip
comments.body: Thanks. Fixed in main since it was a rpython problem and merged to py3.10 in f999cef03ee
